revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岩室温泉]]
{{infobox Onsen|image=Iwamuro-onsen,Niigata-city,Niigata,Japan.jpg}} '''Iwamuro Onsen''' (岩室温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} and a town area located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|qid=Q179691|lt=Niigata City|1=Niigata (city)}}, and {{ill|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|en|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711756|lt=Nishikan Ward|1=Nishikan-ku, Niigata}} (formerly part of the province of {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|qid=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}). Its {{ill|Postal codes in Japan|en|Postal codes in Japan|qid=Q7234027|lt=Postal Code|1=Postal codes in Japan}} is 953-0104<ref name="postal" />.
== History == It is located along the {{ill|Hokkoku Kaidō|ja|北陸道#道（みち）としての北陸道|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|lt_ja=北国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokurikudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3139239}}<ref>[https://n-story.jp/topic/92/page1.php 北国街道と岩室宿の発展] - {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref><ref>[https://www.city.niigata.lg.jp/nishikan/about/kankou/hokoku-nishikan.html 北国街道] - {{ill|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|en|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711756|lt=Nishikan Ward|1=Nishikan-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|qid=Q179691|lt=Niigata City|1=Niigata (city)}}. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref>. The onsen was established in the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, reportedly before the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō Era|1=Keichō}}<ref name="角川地名15、p.191">[[#角川地名15|角川地名15、p.191]]</ref>. According to the {{ill|Onsen Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Founding Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, it is said that a {{ill|Wild Goose|ja|ガン (鳥類)|lt=Wild Goose|lt_ja=雁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Goose|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 雁|1=Goose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16529344}} was discovered healing its wounds in the hot spring. For this reason, it was originally called "Reigan no Yu" and later prospered as a post station for pilgrims visiting {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|ja|弥彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彌彦神社}}.
By a notification from the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Health and Welfare|1=Ministry of Health and Welfare|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q841449}}, on {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|qid=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}}, {{ill|1963|en|1963|qid=Q2755|lt=1963|1=1963}} (Showa 38), it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421080}} together with {{ill|Yahiko Onsen|ja|弥彦温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahiko Onsen|1=Yahiko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487442}}.
When the former {{ill|Iwamuro, Niigata|en|Iwamuro, Niigata|qid=Q6100536|lt=Iwamuro Village|1=Iwamuro, Niigata}} was merged into {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|qid=Q179691|lt=Niigata City|1=Niigata (city)}} on {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|qid=Q2433|lt=March 21|1=March 21}}, {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17), a town name change was implemented, renaming the entire area of the former "Large Section Iwamuro" where Iwamuro Onsen is located to "Iwamuro Onsen".
=== Chronology === * {{ill|1889|en|1889|qid=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22) {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|qid=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}: Became a large section of {{ill|Iwamuro, Niigata|en|Iwamuro, Niigata|qid=Q6100536|lt=Iwamuro Village|1=Iwamuro, Niigata}} through a merger. * {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17) {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|qid=Q2433|lt=March 21|1=March 21}}: Became a large section of {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|qid=Q179691|lt=Niigata City|1=Niigata (city)}} through a merger and was renamed "Iwamuro Onsen". * {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}} (Heisei 19) {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|qid=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}: Became a large section of {{ill|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|en|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711756|lt=Nishikan Ward|1=Nishikan-ku, Niigata}} due to the transition of Niigata City to a [[:en:Cities designated by government ordinance of Japan|Designated City]].
== Onsen Town == The onsen town extends to the north of {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|ja|弥彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彌彦神社}} and on the east side of Taho Mountain. There are 13 establishments of {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|qid=Q562930|lt=Ryokan|1=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|qid=Q27686|lt=Hotel|1=Hotel}}. The onsen ryokan "Takashi no Yado Takashimaya" occasionally attracts events such as the {{ill|Kisei (shogi)|en|Kisei (shogi)|qid=Q3329365|lt=Kisei Championship|1=Kisei (shogi)}} in {{ill|Shogi|en|Shogi|qid=Q131375|lt=Shogi|1=Shogi}} and the {{ill|Jūdan (Go)|en|Jūdan (Go)|qid=Q2635247|lt=Judan Championship|1=Jūdan (Go)}} in {{ill|Go (game)|en|Go (game)|qid=Q11413|lt=Go|1=Go (game)}} as a venue for matches.
Due to its location in the {{ill|Okuzashiki|ja|奥座敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuzashiki|1=okuzashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11445803}} of {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|qid=Q179691|lt=Niigata City|1=Niigata (city)}}, there are also entertainment venues such as {{ill|Snack bar|en|Snack bar|qid=Q1318768|lt=Snack|1=Snack bar}}, and many geisha are employed.
There is one {{ill|Day-Use Bathing Facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bathing Facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, operated by the Niigata City-owned and {{ill|Designated Manager|ja|指定管理者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated Manager|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11497972}}-managed "Niigata City Iwamuro Health Promotion Center" (nicknamed "Yuugan no Yu Yorinare").
In addition, adjacent to the onsen town, there is also a day-use bathing facility at {{ill|Taho Onsen|ja|多宝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505752|1=Taho Onsen}}.
In the onsen town, {{ill|Bike rental|en|Bike rental|qid=Q10611118|lt=Rental Bicycle|1=Bike rental}} services are available (for a fee)<ref>[https://www.city.niigata.lg.jp/miryoku/sukima_time/1day/iwamuroonsen.html How to Enjoy Niigata City's Hidden Gem: Iwamuro Onsen Rental Cycle Course] - {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|qid=Q179691|lt=Niigata City|1=Niigata (city)}}. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref>.
=== Water Quality === * Sodium - Chloride Hot Spring ** {{ill|Spring Source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}{{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|qid=Q11466|lt=Temperature|1=Temperature}} approximately 41°C
== Transportation == [[File:Linemap of Buses in Niigata Nishikan.svg|thumb|300px|Bus route map. The yellow route in the lower left is the nearest one.]] ; Public Transportation * From either {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} ({{ill|Japan Railways Group|en|Japan Railways Group|qid=Q864495|lt=JR|1=Japan Railways Group}}) on the {{ill|Echigo Line|en|Echigo Line|qid=Q188250|lt=Echigo Line|1=Echigo Line}}, from {{ill|Maki Station (Niigata)|en|Maki Station (Niigata)|qid=Q6739504|lt=Maki Station|1=Maki Station (Niigata)}} or {{ill|Iwamuro Station|en|Iwamuro Station|qid=Q11474353|lt=Iwamuro Station|1=Iwamuro Station}}, take the {{ill|West Sightseeing Bus|ja|ウエスト観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West Sightseeing Bus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} bound for "Iwamuro/Mase" and alight at "Iwamuro"<ref>[http://westbus.net/rocalbus.html Route Bus] - West Sightseeing Bus. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref>. *: Travel time: Approximately 25 minutes from {{ill|Maki Station (Niigata)|en|Maki Station (Niigata)|qid=Q6739504|lt=Maki Station|1=Maki Station (Niigata)}} and about 10 minutes from {{ill|Iwamuro Station|en|Iwamuro Station|qid=Q11474353|lt=Iwamuro Station|1=Iwamuro Station}}. * From {{ill|Maki Station (Niigata)|en|Maki Station (Niigata)|qid=Q6739504|lt=Maki Station|1=Maki Station (Niigata)}} or {{ill|Yahiko Line|en|Yahiko Line|qid=Q1043958|lt=Yahiko Line|1=Yahiko Line}} {{ill|Yahiko Station|en|Yahiko Station|qid=Q8046814|lt=Yahiko Station|1=Yahiko Station}}, or from surrounding tourist areas, the "Nishikan Sightseeing Round Bus" is available (operated seasonally, mainly on weekends)<ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://www.niigatawestcoast.jp/bus|title=Nishikan Sightseeing Round Bus|publisher=Niigata West Coast|accessdate=2020-09-12}}</ref>. * Approximately 10 minutes by {{ill|Taxi|en|Taxi|qid=Q82650|lt=Taxi|1=Taxi}} from {{ill|Iwamuro Station|en|Iwamuro Station|qid=Q11474353|lt=Iwamuro Station|1=Iwamuro Station}} on the {{ill|Echigo Line|en|Echigo Line|qid=Q188250|lt=Echigo Line|1=Echigo Line}}. * From {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|qid=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport|1=Niigata Airport}}, you can use the reservation-based {{ill|Share taxi|en|Share taxi|qid=Q842389|lt=Shared Taxi|1=Share taxi}} "Niigata West Coastliner"<ref>[https://www.iwamuroya.com/nwc/ Reservation for Niigata West Coastliner] - Iwamuroya. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|From the same airport, tour operations may also be conducted as part of WILLER's Japan Sea Transverse Sightseeing Route Project<ref>[https://japansea.jp/iwamuro.html Iwamuro Onsen Direct Access] - Japan Sea Transverse Sightseeing Route Project. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref>}}. * From {{ill|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|en|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|qid=Q22123549|lt=Busta Shinjuku|1=Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Willer Express|en|Willer Express|qid=Q11253679|lt=WILLER EXPRESS|1=Willer Express}} H5102 service and alight at "Iwamuro Onsen Yumotoya"{{Refnest|group="Note"|In operation since {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}} (from {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|qid=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}})<ref>[https://lightseeing2011.blogspot.com/2015/07/ Iwamuro Onsen ⇔ Tokyo Express Bus Service Launched!] - Iwamuroya Tourism Blog. Accessed June 23, 2019.</ref>. Initially, one round-trip service operated from {{ill|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|en|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|qid=Q22123549|lt=Busta Shinjuku|1=Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal}} (until {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} (until {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}, when it was known as WBT Shinjuku)) and from {{ill|Tokyo Station|en|Tokyo Station|qid=Q283196|lt=Tokyo Station|1=Tokyo Station}}.}}. For the return trip, the H5205 service bound for {{ill|Tokyo Station|en|Tokyo Station|qid=Q283196|lt=Tokyo Station|1=Tokyo Station}} (Yaesu South Exit) is available. *: Travel time: Approximately 5 hours 25 minutes from {{ill|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|en|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|qid=Q22123549|lt=Busta Shinjuku|1=Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal}} and about 5 hours 40 minutes to {{ill|Tokyo Station|en|Tokyo Station|qid=Q283196|lt=Tokyo Station|1=Tokyo Station}}. *: Number of services: 1 service daily from {{ill|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|en|Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal|qid=Q22123549|lt=Busta Shinjuku|1=Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal}} and 1 service daily to {{ill|Tokyo Station|en|Tokyo Station|qid=Q283196|lt=Tokyo Station|1=Tokyo Station}}. ; By Car * Approximately 15 minutes by car via {{ill|Japan National Route 460|en|Japan National Route 460|qid=Q11421467|lt=National Route 460|1=Japan National Route 460}} and {{ill|Maki Minami Bypass|ja|巻南バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maki Minami Bypass|1=Q17221851|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17221851}} from {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|qid=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}} {{ill|Makigata Higashi Interchange|ja|巻潟東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makigata Higashi Interchange|1=Maki-Katahigashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10910772}}.
== Number of Households and Population == As of {{ill|January 31|en|January 31|qid=Q2289|lt=January 31|1=January 31}}, {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} (Heisei 30), the number of households and population are as follows<ref name="population" />. {| class="wikitable" !{{ill|Large Section|ja|大字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large Section|1=ōaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q424857}}!!{{ill|Households|ja|世帯数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Households|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}!!{{ill|Population|ja|人口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Population|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Human population|qid=Q33829}} |- |Iwamuro Onsen |style="text-align:right"|282 households |style="text-align:right"|674 people |}
== Elementary and Junior High School Districts == For those attending municipal elementary and junior high schools, the school district is as follows<ref>{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=http://www.city.niigata.lg.jp/kosodate/gakko/sho_chu_school/tsugakukuiki/sub022/index.html|title=School District List (Alphabetical by Town Name)|publisher=Niigata City|date=2017-04-01|accessdate=2018-03-05}}</ref>. {| class="wikitable" !{{ill|House Number|ja|番地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House Number|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地番|1=Land lot|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q683595}}!!{{ill|Primary school|en|Primary school|qid=Q9842|lt=Elementary School|1=Primary school}}!!{{ill|Junior High School|ja|中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior High School|1=lower secondary school in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55521176}} |- ||Entire Area||{{ill|Niigata City Iwamuro Elementary School|ja|新潟市立岩室小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Iwamuro Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||{{ill|Niigata City Iwamuro Junior High School|ja|新潟市立岩室中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Iwamuro Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |}
== Footnotes == ; Notes {{Reflist|group="Note"}} ; Sources
{{Reflist}}
== References == *{{Cite book|language=Japanese |author = Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee |date = 1989-10-08 |year = |title = Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 15: Niigata Prefecture |publisher = (株)角川書店 |isbn = 4-04-001150-3 |ref = 角川地名15 }}
=== Related Topics === * {{ill|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|en|Nishikan-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711756|lt=Nishikan Ward|1=Nishikan-ku, Niigata}}
== Surroundings == * {{ill|Taho Onsen|ja|多宝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505752|1=Taho Onsen}} * {{ill|Natsui no Hazagi|ja|夏井のハザ木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsui no Hazagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kakudayama|ja|角田山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakudayama|1=Mount Kakuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630786}} * {{ill|Mount Yahiko|en|Mount Yahiko|qid=Q152041|lt=Yahiko Mountain|1=Mount Yahiko}}・{{ill|Yahiko Onsen|ja|弥彦温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahiko Onsen|1=Yahiko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487442}} * {{ill|Kamizakigata|ja|上堰潟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizakigata|1=Uwasekigata Lagoon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60637557}}
== External Links == * [https://www.iwamurokankou.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Iwamuro Onsen Tourism Association|ja|岩室温泉観光協会}]] ** [https://www.iwamurokankou.com/images/sightseeing/tourist_map.pdf Broad Area Tourism Guide Map]] * [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Ministry of the Environment: National Health Resorts|ja|環境省：国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Environment: National Health Resorts|qq=}}] ( [https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_025.pdf {{ill|Iwamuro Onsen National Health Resort Plan|ja|岩室温泉国民保養温泉地計画書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamuro Onsen National Health Resort Plan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] )
